title: The Door In The Dark
content:
In going from room to room in the dark,
I reached out blindly to save my face,
But neglected, however lightly, to lace
My fingers and close my arms in an arc.
A slim door got in past my guard,
And hit me a blow in the head so hard
I had my native simile jarred.
So people and things don't pair any more
With what they used to pair with before.
meaning:
Robert Lee Frost's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Door In The Dark"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
